# AI Email Outreach Platform — Backend Specification

> **Version:** 1.0 (v1 scope)
> **Stack:** Python 3.12 · FastAPI · PostgreSQL · Poetry

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Tech Stack & Rationale](#1-tech-stack--rationale)
2. [Project Structure](#2-project-structure)
3. [Poetry Setup & Dependency Files](#3-poetry-setup--dependency-files)
4. [Environment Variables](#4-environment-variables)
5. [Design Principles](#5-design-principles)
6. [Services Architecture](#6-services-architecture)
7. [Database Schema](#7-database-schema)
8. [LLM Provider Service (Centralized)](#8-llm-provider-service-centralized)
9. [Background Tasks Architecture](#9-background-tasks-architecture)
10. [Error Response Standard](#10-error-response-standard)
11. [Build & Run Instructions](#11-build--run-instructions)

> **API Contracts:** See [`contracts.md`](./contracts.md)

---

## 1. Tech Stack & Rationale

| Layer | Choice | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Language | Python 3.12 | Best LLM/AI library ecosystem |
| Web Framework | FastAPI | Async-first, auto OpenAPI docs, Pydantic validation |
| ORM | SQLAlchemy 2.0 (async) | Type-safe, async sessions, migration support |
| Migrations | Alembic | Industry standard with SQLAlchemy |
| Database | PostgreSQL 15 | JSONB for config blobs, reliable, relational |
| Background Jobs | FastAPI BackgroundTasks | In-process async tasks — no broker needed for v1 |
| Package Manager | Poetry | Deterministic lockfile, dev/prod split |
| HTTP Client | httpx | Async, used for LLM & external API calls |
| Validation | Pydantic v2 | Schema validation, structured LLM output parsing |
| Auth | python-jose + passlib | JWT tokens |
| Testing | pytest + pytest-asyncio | Async test support |

**v1 intentional omissions:** Redis, ARQ, BullMQ, Celery. A job broker adds operational overhead that isn't justified until campaign volumes exceed single-process capacity. FastAPI `BackgroundTasks` runs in the same process, keeps the stack boring, and is trivially swappable for ARQ in v2 behind the same service interface.

---

## 2. Project Structure

```
outreach-backend/
├── pyproject.toml
├── poetry.lock
├── requirements.txt            # Exported prod deps (for Docker)
├── requirements-dev.txt
├── .env.example
├── alembic.ini
├── alembic/
│   └── versions/
├── app/
│   ├── main.py                 # FastAPI app entry point
│   ├── config.py               # Settings (pydantic-settings)
│   ├── deps.py                 # Shared FastAPI dependencies (DB session, auth)
│   │
│   ├── api/                    # Route handlers — thin controllers only
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   └── v1/
│   │       ├── __init__.py
│   │       ├── auth.py
│   │       ├── campaigns.py
│   │       ├── chat.py
│   │       ├── outreach.py
│   │       └── email_jobs.py
│   │
│   ├── services/               # Business logic — one class per domain (SRP)
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── campaign_service.py
│   │   ├── chat_service.py
│   │   ├── llm_service.py      # Centralized LLM provider (strategy pattern)
│   │   ├── processor_service.py
│   │   ├── email_service.py
│   │   ├── apollo_service.py
│   │   ├── scraper_service.py
│   │   └── google_drive_service.py
│   │
│   ├── interfaces/             # Abstract base classes (DIP, ISP)
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── llm_provider.py     # AbstractLLMProvider
│   │   ├── email_provider.py   # AbstractEmailProvider

## 5. Design Principles

This codebase enforces SOLID throughout. Each layer has one job; new providers never require touching existing code.

### Single Responsibility (SRP)

Every class owns exactly one domain concern:

```
CampaignService   → campaign lifecycle only
ChatService       → wizard conversation only
LLMService        → provider routing + retry only
ProcessorService  → research + draft orchestration only
EmailService      → send + tracking only
```

Route handlers in `api/v1/` are thin controllers: parse request → call service → return response. No business logic in routes.

### Open / Closed (OCP)

New LLM providers, email providers, and contact sources are added by creating a new file under `providers/` implementing the relevant abstract interface. Nothing in `services/` or `api/` changes.

```
AbstractLLMProvider
  ├── OpenAIProvider
  ├── AnthropicProvider
  └── GeminiProvider          ← add new provider here, nowhere else

AbstractEmailProvider
  └── GmailProvider           ← SendGrid in v2: new file, no edits

AbstractContactSource
  ├── ApolloProvider
  └── ScraperProvider
```

### Liskov Substitution (LSP)

All providers implement the full interface contract. `LLMService` accepts `AbstractLLMProvider` — any concrete provider can be injected without changing the call site.

### Interface Segregation (ISP)

Interfaces are narrow and focused:

```python
# app/interfaces/llm_provider.py
class AbstractLLMProvider(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    async def chat_completion(self, messages: list, system_prompt: str) -> str: ...

    @abstractmethod
    async def structured_completion(self, messages: list, system_prompt: str) -> dict: ...
```

Contact sources and email providers each have their own minimal interface — no god interfaces.

### Dependency Inversion (DIP)

Services depend on abstractions. Concrete implementations are injected via FastAPI `Depends()`:

```python
# app/deps.py
async def get_llm_service(settings: Settings = Depends(get_settings)) -> LLMService:
    provider = ProviderFactory.create(settings.DEFAULT_LLM_PROVIDER)
    return LLMService(provider=provider)
```

### Repository Pattern

All DB access goes through repositories. Services never call SQLAlchemy directly:

```python
# app/repositories/base.py
class AsyncBaseRepository(Generic[T]):
    def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession, model: type[T]) -> None:
        self.session = session
        self.model = model

    async def get_by_id(self, id: UUID) -> T | None:
        result = await self.session.execute(select(self.model).where(self.model.id == id))
        return result.scalar_one_or_none()

    async def create(self, obj: T) -> T:
        self.session.add(obj)
        await self.session.flush()
        await self.session.refresh(obj)
        return obj

    async def update(self, obj: T) -> T:
        await self.session.flush()
        await self.session.refresh(obj)
        return obj

    async def bulk_create(self, objs: list[T]) -> list[T]:
        self.session.add_all(objs)
        await self.session.flush()
        return objs
```

### Async All the Way Down

No synchronous blocking anywhere in the request path or background tasks:

- DB: `AsyncSession` + `asyncpg` driver — all queries are `await session.execute(...)`
- HTTP: `httpx.AsyncClient` for all LLM and external API calls
- Background tasks: `async def` task functions registered with FastAPI `BackgroundTasks`
- File I/O (if needed): `aiofiles`
- Concurrency in processor: `asyncio.gather()` with semaphore for bounded parallelism

```python
# Correct — always this pattern
async def get_campaign(self, campaign_id: UUID, user_id: UUID) -> Campaign:
    return await self.campaign_repo.get_by_id_for_user(campaign_id, user_id)

# Never this
def get_campaign(self, campaign_id: UUID, user_id: UUID) -> Campaign:
    return db.query(Campaign).filter(...).first()
```

### Abstract Interface

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

class AbstractLLMProvider(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    async def chat_completion(
        self,
        messages: list[dict],
        system_prompt: str,
        model: str,
    ) -> str: ...

        self,
        messages: list[dict],

    ) -> dict: ...
```

### Prompt Templates

```

### Structured Output Pattern

```python
async def extract_params(self, messages: list, campaign_type: str) -> ExtractedParams:
    response = await self.provider.structured_completion(
        messages=messages,
        system_prompt=EXTRACTION_PROMPT,
        model=self.model,
    )
    try:
        return ExtractedParams(**response)
    except ValidationError:
        return await self._retry_with_correction(messages, response)
```

### Fallback Chain

```
Primary provider (user setting)
  → on 429/500: wait 1s, retry once (tenacity)
  → on continued failure: switch to DEFAULT_LLM_PROVIDER
  → on complete failure: raise LLMUnavailableError → 503
```

## 9. Background Tasks Architecture

v1 uses FastAPI's built-in `BackgroundTasks`. No broker, no worker process, no Redis.

### How it works

```
POST /campaigns/{id}/sessions  (finalize session)
  │
  ├── Save extracted_params to DB (awaited)
  ├── Set campaign.status = "processing" (awaited)
  └── background_tasks.add_task(run_processor, campaign_id, user_id, db_factory)
      │
      └── [runs after response is sent, in same process event loop]
          ├── Load campaign + session from DB (async)
          ├── Fetch contacts via Apollo or Scraper (async httpx)
          ├── Bulk insert contacts to DB (async)
          ├── For each contact (bounded by asyncio.Semaphore):
          │     └── LLMService.generate_email() (async)
          │     └── Insert EmailJob to DB (async)
          │     └── Append SSE event to in-process queue
          └── Set campaign.status = "ready" (async)
```

### In-process SSE

Without Redis, SSE progress events use a per-campaign `asyncio.Queue` stored in an in-process registry:

```python
# app/core/sse.py
from asyncio import Queue
from collections import defaultdict

_queues: dict[str, list[Queue]] = defaultdict(list)

def subscribe(campaign_id: str) -> Queue:
    q: Queue = Queue()
    _queues[campaign_id].append(q)
    return q

async def publish(campaign_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
    for q in _queues.get(campaign_id, []):
        await q.put(event)

def unsubscribe(campaign_id: str, q: Queue) -> None:
    _queues[campaign_id].remove(q)
```

**v1 constraint:** SSE only works correctly with a single-process uvicorn deployment. This is acceptable for v1. In v2, swap `_queues` for Redis pub-sub behind the same `publish`/`subscribe` interface — no call-site changes needed.

### Processor Task

```python
# app/tasks/processor_task.py
async def run_processor(
    campaign_id: UUID,
    user_id: UUID,
    db_factory: Callable[[], AsyncSession],
) -> None:
    async with db_factory() as session:
        campaign_repo = CampaignRepository(session)
        contact_repo = ContactRepository(session)
        email_job_repo = EmailJobRepository(session)

        campaign = await campaign_repo.get_by_id(campaign_id)
        session_obj = await campaign_repo.get_session(campaign_id)
        params = session_obj.extracted_params

        contact_source = ContactSourceFactory.create(params["email_source"])
        contacts = await contact_source.search(params)
        await contact_repo.bulk_create(contacts)

        sem = asyncio.Semaphore(settings.PROCESSOR_MAX_CONCURRENCY)

        async def process_one(contact: Contact) -> None:
            async with sem:
                draft = await llm_service.generate_email(contact, session_obj, user_profile)
                job = await email_job_repo.create(EmailJob(**draft, contact_id=contact.id))
                await publish(str(campaign_id), {"type": "draft_ready", "email_job_id": str(job.id)})

        await asyncio.gather(*[process_one(c) for c in contacts])
        await campaign_repo.update_status(campaign_id, "ready")
        await publish(str(campaign_id), {"type": "complete", "total": len(contacts)})
```

### Email Task

```python
# app/tasks/email_task.py
async def send_email_job(
    email_job_id: UUID,
    db_factory: Callable[[], AsyncSession],
) -> None:
    async with db_factory() as session:
        email_job_repo = EmailJobRepository(session)
        job = await email_job_repo.get_by_id(email_job_id)

        daily_ok = await email_service.check_daily_limit(job.user_id, session)
        if not daily_ok:
            await email_job_repo.update_status(job.id, "queued")
            return

        try:
            result = await email_service.send_via_gmail(job)
            await email_job_repo.mark_sent(job.id, result.message_id)
        except BounceError:
            await email_job_repo.update_status(job.id, "bounced")
        except Exception as e:
            await email_job_repo.mark_failed(job.id, str(e))
```

## 10. Error Response Standard

All errors return this shape:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "CAMPAIGN_NOT_FOUND",
    "message": "Campaign with ID abc123 not found",
    "details": null
  }
}
```

### Error Codes

| HTTP Status | Code | When |
|---|---|---|
| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Request body fails Pydantic schema |
| 401 | `UNAUTHORIZED` | Missing or expired JWT |
| 403 | `FORBIDDEN` | User doesn't own the resource |
| 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | Resource doesn't exist |
| 409 | `CONFLICT` | Duplicate resource |
| 422 | `UNPROCESSABLE` | Business logic validation failed |
| 429 | `RATE_LIMITED` | Too many requests |
| 503 | `LLM_UNAVAILABLE` | All LLM providers failed |
| 500 | `INTERNAL_ERROR` | Unexpected server error |

### Bounce Warning (informational, not error)

```json
{
  "outreaches": [],
  "_warnings": [
    {
      "code": "BOUNCE_RISK",
      "message": "12 contacts were found via web scraping. Email addresses may be inaccurate."
    }
  ]
}
```

# AI Email Outreach Platform — Backend Specification

> **Version:** 1.0 (v1 scope)
> **Stack:** Python 3.12 · FastAPI · PostgreSQL · Poetry

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Tech Stack & Rationale](#1-tech-stack--rationale)
2. [Project Structure](#2-project-structure)
3. [Poetry Setup & Dependency Files](#3-poetry-setup--dependency-files)
4. [Environment Variables](#4-environment-variables)
5. [Design Principles](#5-design-principles)
6. [Services Architecture](#6-services-architecture)

9. [Background Tasks Architecture](#9-background-tasks-architecture)

11. [Build & Run Instructions](#11-build--run-instructions)

> **API Contracts:** See [`contracts.md`](./contracts.md)

## 1. Tech Stack & Rationale

| Layer | Choice | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Language | Python 3.12 | Best LLM/AI library ecosystem |
| Web Framework | FastAPI | Async-first, auto OpenAPI docs, Pydantic validation |
| ORM | SQLAlchemy 2.0 (async) | Type-safe, async sessions, migration support |
| Migrations | Alembic | Industry standard with SQLAlchemy |
| Database | PostgreSQL 15 | JSONB for config blobs, reliable, relational |
| Background Jobs | FastAPI BackgroundTasks | In-process async tasks — no broker needed for v1 |
| Package Manager | Poetry | Deterministic lockfile, dev/prod split |
| HTTP Client | httpx | Async, used for LLM & external API calls |
| Validation | Pydantic v2 | Schema validation, structured LLM output parsing |
| Auth | python-jose + passlib | JWT tokens |
| Testing | pytest + pytest-asyncio | Async test support |

**v1 intentional omissions:** Redis, ARQ, BullMQ, Celery. A job broker adds operational overhead that isn't justified until campaign volumes exceed single-process capacity. FastAPI `BackgroundTasks` runs in the same process, keeps the stack boring, and is trivially swappable for ARQ in v2 behind the same service interface.

## 5. Design Principles

This codebase enforces SOLID throughout. Each layer has one job; new providers never require touching existing code.

### Single Responsibility (SRP)

Every class owns exactly one domain concern:

```
CampaignService   → campaign lifecycle only
ChatService       → wizard conversation only
LLMService        → provider routing + retry only
ProcessorService  → research + draft orchestration only
EmailService      → send + tracking only
```

Route handlers in `api/v1/` are thin controllers: parse request → call service → return response. No business logic in routes.

### Open / Closed (OCP)

New LLM providers, email providers, and contact sources are added by creating a new file under `providers/` implementing the relevant abstract interface. Nothing in `services/` or `api/` changes.

```
AbstractLLMProvider
  ├── OpenAIProvider
  ├── AnthropicProvider
  └── GeminiProvider          ← add new provider here, nowhere else

AbstractEmailProvider
  └── GmailProvider           ← SendGrid in v2: new file, no edits

AbstractContactSource
  ├── ApolloProvider
  └── ScraperProvider
```

### Liskov Substitution (LSP)

All providers implement the full interface contract. `LLMService` accepts `AbstractLLMProvider` — any concrete provider can be injected without changing the call site.

### Interface Segregation (ISP)

Interfaces are narrow and focused:

```python
# app/interfaces/llm_provider.py
class AbstractLLMProvider(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    async def chat_completion(self, messages: list, system_prompt: str) -> str: ...

    @abstractmethod
    async def structured_completion(self, messages: list, system_prompt: str) -> dict: ...
```

Contact sources and email providers each have their own minimal interface — no god interfaces.

### Dependency Inversion (DIP)

Services depend on abstractions. Concrete implementations are injected via FastAPI `Depends()`:

```python
# app/deps.py
async def get_llm_service(settings: Settings = Depends(get_settings)) -> LLMService:
    provider = ProviderFactory.create(settings.DEFAULT_LLM_PROVIDER)
    return LLMService(provider=provider)
```

### Repository Pattern

All DB access goes through repositories. Services never call SQLAlchemy directly:

```python
# app/repositories/base.py
class AsyncBaseRepository(Generic[T]):
    def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession, model: type[T]) -> None:
        self.session = session
        self.model = model

    async def get_by_id(self, id: UUID) -> T | None:
        result = await self.session.execute(select(self.model).where(self.model.id == id))
        return result.scalar_one_or_none()

    async def create(self, obj: T) -> T:
        self.session.add(obj)
        await self.session.flush()
        await self.session.refresh(obj)
        return obj

    async def update(self, obj: T) -> T:
        await self.session.flush()
        await self.session.refresh(obj)
        return obj

    async def bulk_create(self, objs: list[T]) -> list[T]:
        self.session.add_all(objs)
        await self.session.flush()
        return objs
```

### Async All the Way Down

No synchronous blocking anywhere in the request path or background tasks:

- DB: `AsyncSession` + `asyncpg` driver — all queries are `await session.execute(...)`
- HTTP: `httpx.AsyncClient` for all LLM and external API calls
- Background tasks: `async def` task functions registered with FastAPI `BackgroundTasks`
- File I/O (if needed): `aiofiles`
- Concurrency in processor: `asyncio.gather()` with semaphore for bounded parallelism

```python
# Correct — always this pattern
async def get_campaign(self, campaign_id: UUID, user_id: UUID) -> Campaign:
    return await self.campaign_repo.get_by_id_for_user(campaign_id, user_id)

# Never this
def get_campaign(self, campaign_id: UUID, user_id: UUID) -> Campaign:
    return db.query(Campaign).filter(...).first()
```

  ├── async get_campaign(campaign_id, user_id) → Campaign

ChatService

  ├── async save_session(session_id, extracted_params) → CampaignSession
  └── async get_session(session_id) → CampaignSession

LLMService  ← CENTRALIZED. Used by ChatService AND ProcessorService
  ├── async chat_completion(messages, provider, model, system_prompt) → str
  ├── async generate_email(contact, campaign_session, user_profile) → EmailDraft
  └── async extract_params(raw_message, campaign_type) → ExtractedParams

ProcessorService  ← runs inside BackgroundTask
  ├── async run_research(campaign_id) → list[Contact]
  ├── async dispatch_agents(contacts, campaign_session, progress_cb) → None
  └── async generate_all_drafts(contacts, campaign_session) → list[EmailJob]

ApolloService

## 8. LLM Provider Service (Centralized)

Single entry point for all LLM calls. Route handlers never call LLM directly — only services do, and only through `LLMService`.

### Abstract Interface

```python
# app/interfaces/llm_provider.py
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

class AbstractLLMProvider(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    async def chat_completion(
        self,
        messages: list[dict],
        system_prompt: str,
        model: str,
    ) -> str: ...

    @abstractmethod
    async def structured_completion(
        self,
        messages: list[dict],
        system_prompt: str,
        model: str,
    ) -> dict: ...
```

### Modes

```python
# app/services/llm_service.py
class LLMMode(str, Enum):
    CHAT = "chat"             # ChatService wizard conversation
    GENERATION = "generation" # ProcessorService email drafts
    EXTRACTION = "extraction" # ChatService param parsing
```

### Prompt Templates

```
app/prompts/
├── chat_base.txt
├── extraction.txt
├── generation_job.txt
├── generation_b2b.txt
├── generation_hiring.txt
└── generation_pr.txt
```

### Structured Output Pattern

```python
async def extract_params(self, messages: list, campaign_type: str) -> ExtractedParams:
    response = await self.provider.structured_completion(
        messages=messages,
        system_prompt=EXTRACTION_PROMPT,
        model=self.model,
    )
    try:
        return ExtractedParams(**response)
    except ValidationError:
        return await self._retry_with_correction(messages, response)
```

### Fallback Chain

```
Primary provider (user setting)
  → on 429/500: wait 1s, retry once (tenacity)
  → on continued failure: switch to DEFAULT_LLM_PROVIDER
  → on complete failure: raise LLMUnavailableError → 503
```

---

## 9. Background Tasks Architecture

v1 uses FastAPI's built-in `BackgroundTasks`. No broker, no worker process, no Redis.

### How it works

```
POST /campaigns/{id}/sessions  (finalize session)
  │
  ├── Save extracted_params to DB (awaited)
  ├── Set campaign.status = "processing" (awaited)
  └── background_tasks.add_task(run_processor, campaign_id, user_id, db_factory)
      │
      └── [runs after response is sent, in same process event loop]
          ├── Load campaign + session from DB (async)
          ├── Fetch contacts via Apollo or Scraper (async httpx)
          ├── Bulk insert contacts to DB (async)
          ├── For each contact (bounded by asyncio.Semaphore):
          │     └── LLMService.generate_email() (async)
          │     └── Insert EmailJob to DB (async)
          │     └── Append SSE event to in-process queue
          └── Set campaign.status = "ready" (async)
```

### In-process SSE

Without Redis, SSE progress events use a per-campaign `asyncio.Queue` stored in an in-process registry:

```python
# app/core/sse.py
from asyncio import Queue
from collections import defaultdict

_queues: dict[str, list[Queue]] = defaultdict(list)

def subscribe(campaign_id: str) -> Queue:
    q: Queue = Queue()
    _queues[campaign_id].append(q)
    return q

async def publish(campaign_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
    for q in _queues.get(campaign_id, []):
        await q.put(event)

def unsubscribe(campaign_id: str, q: Queue) -> None:
    _queues[campaign_id].remove(q)
```

**v1 constraint:** SSE only works correctly with a single-process uvicorn deployment. This is acceptable for v1. In v2, swap `_queues` for Redis pub-sub behind the same `publish`/`subscribe` interface — no call-site changes needed.

### Processor Task

```python
# app/tasks/processor_task.py
async def run_processor(
    campaign_id: UUID,
    user_id: UUID,
    db_factory: Callable[[], AsyncSession],
) -> None:
    async with db_factory() as session:
        campaign_repo = CampaignRepository(session)
        contact_repo = ContactRepository(session)
        email_job_repo = EmailJobRepository(session)

        campaign = await campaign_repo.get_by_id(campaign_id)
        session_obj = await campaign_repo.get_session(campaign_id)
        params = session_obj.extracted_params

        contact_source = ContactSourceFactory.create(params["email_source"])
        contacts = await contact_source.search(params)
        await contact_repo.bulk_create(contacts)

        sem = asyncio.Semaphore(settings.PROCESSOR_MAX_CONCURRENCY)

        async def process_one(contact: Contact) -> None:
            async with sem:
                draft = await llm_service.generate_email(contact, session_obj, user_profile)
                job = await email_job_repo.create(EmailJob(**draft, contact_id=contact.id))
                await publish(str(campaign_id), {"type": "draft_ready", "email_job_id": str(job.id)})

        await asyncio.gather(*[process_one(c) for c in contacts])
        await campaign_repo.update_status(campaign_id, "ready")
        await publish(str(campaign_id), {"type": "complete", "total": len(contacts)})
```

### Email Task

```python
# app/tasks/email_task.py
async def send_email_job(
    email_job_id: UUID,
    db_factory: Callable[[], AsyncSession],
) -> None:
    async with db_factory() as session:
        email_job_repo = EmailJobRepository(session)
        job = await email_job_repo.get_by_id(email_job_id)

        daily_ok = await email_service.check_daily_limit(job.user_id, session)
        if not daily_ok:
            await email_job_repo.update_status(job.id, "queued")
            return

        try:
            result = await email_service.send_via_gmail(job)
            await email_job_repo.mark_sent(job.id, result.message_id)
        except BounceError:
            await email_job_repo.update_status(job.id, "bounced")
        except Exception as e:
            await email_job_repo.mark_failed(job.id, str(e))
```

---

## 10. Error Response Standard

All errors return this shape:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "CAMPAIGN_NOT_FOUND",
    "message": "Campaign with ID abc123 not found",
    "details": null
  }
}
```

### Error Codes

| HTTP Status | Code | When |
|---|---|---|
| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Request body fails Pydantic schema |
| 401 | `UNAUTHORIZED` | Missing or expired JWT |
| 403 | `FORBIDDEN` | User doesn't own the resource |
| 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | Resource doesn't exist |
| 409 | `CONFLICT` | Duplicate resource |
| 422 | `UNPROCESSABLE` | Business logic validation failed |
| 429 | `RATE_LIMITED` | Too many requests |
| 503 | `LLM_UNAVAILABLE` | All LLM providers failed |
| 500 | `INTERNAL_ERROR` | Unexpected server error |

### Bounce Warning (informational, not error)

```json
{
  "outreaches": [],
  "_warnings": [
    {
      "code": "BOUNCE_RISK",
      "message": "12 contacts were found via web scraping. Email addresses may be inaccurate."
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 11. Build & Run Instructions

### First-time Setup

```bash
# 1. Install Poetry
curl -sSL https://install.python-poetry.org | python3 -

# 2. Install dependencies
poetry install

# 3. Copy and fill env vars
cp .env.example .env

# 4. Start PostgreSQL (Docker)
docker compose up -d db

# 5. Run migrations
poetry run alembic upgrade head

# 6. Start the API server
poetry run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
```

> **v1:** No Redis, no separate worker process. One command starts everything.

### Docker Compose (`docker-compose.yml`)

```yaml
version: "3.9"
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:15
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: outreach
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: outreach
      POSTGRES_DB: outreach_db
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data

volumes:
  pgdata:
```

### Migrations

```bash
poetry run alembic revision --autogenerate -m "describe change"
poetry run alembic upgrade head
poetry run alembic downgrade -1
```

### Tests

```bash
poetry run pytest
poetry run pytest --cov=app --cov-report=html
poetry run pytest tests/test_campaigns.py -v
```

### Linting

```bash
poetry run ruff check .
poetry run ruff format .
poetry run mypy app/
```

| **P0** | `POST /campaigns` + `GET /campaigns` | Core resource |
| **P0** | ChatService + LLMService (chat mode) | Core wizard flow |
| **P0** | `POST /campaigns/{id}/chat` (SSE stream) | Wizard UX |
| **P1** | `PUT /campaigns/{id}/sessions` → enqueue BackgroundTask | Triggers research |
| **P1** | ProcessorTask (Apollo path first) | Research pipeline |
| **P1** | LLMService (generation mode) + email prompts | Draft generation |
| **P1** | `GET /campaigns/{id}/outreach` | Dashboard data |

| **P2** | EmailTask + Gmail send | Actual sending |
| **P2** | `GET /campaigns/{id}/stats` | Tracking |
| **P2** | Scraper path + bounce warnings | Contact source fallback |
| **P3** | Swap in-process SSE for Redis pub-sub | Multi-process readiness |
| **P3** | Swap BackgroundTasks for ARQ | Scale beyond single process |
| **P3** | SendGrid integration | v2 email provider |

# AI Email Outreach Platform — API Contracts

> **Version:** 1.0 (v1 scope)
> All endpoints prefixed `/api/v1`.
> Auth: `Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>` header on all protected routes.
> Content-Type: `application/json` for all request bodies.
> The current user is always derived from the token — never from the request body.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Auth](#1-auth)
2. [Campaigns](#2-campaigns)
3. [Chat (Wizard)](#3-chat-wizard)
4. [Outreach Dashboard](#4-outreach-dashboard)
5. [Email Jobs (Send)](#5-email-jobs-send)

---

## 1. Auth

### `POST /api/v1/auth/google`

Initiate Google OAuth flow.

**Request:** `{}`

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "auth_url": "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth?..."
}
```

---

### `GET /api/v1/auth/google/callback`

Google OAuth redirect handler.

**Query params:** `code`, `state`

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "access_token": "eyJ...",
  "token_type": "bearer",
  "user": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "email": "user@example.com"
  }
}
```

---

## 2. Campaigns

### `POST /api/v1/campaigns`

Create a new campaign and start the chat wizard session.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "type": "job_outreach"
}
```

`type` enum: `job_outreach | b2b | hiring | pr | fundraising | partnership`

**Response `201`:**
```json
{
  "campaign_id": "uuid",
  "session_id": "uuid",
  "status": "draft",
  "first_message": "I'd love to help you with your job outreach campaign! Let's start — what role are you targeting and which companies interest you?"
}
```

---

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns`

List all campaigns for the authenticated user.

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "campaigns": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "type": "job_outreach",
      "status": "ready",
      "created_at": "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "total": 5
}
```

---

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}`

Get campaign detail.

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "type": "b2b",
  "status": "processing",
  "config_json": {},
  "created_at": "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z"
}
```

---

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/stats`

Aggregate stats for a campaign.

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "total_contacts": 45,
  "total_drafts": 45,
  "approved": 43,
  "queued": 2,
  "sent": 38,
  "opened": 12,
  "replied": 3,
  "bounced": 2,
  "failed": 1
}
```

---

## 3. Chat (Wizard)

### `POST /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/chat`

Send a chat message to the campaign wizard. Returns a streaming SSE response.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "message": "I want to reach out to HR managers at Series B SaaS companies in the US"
}
```

**Response:** `text/event-stream`

```
data: {"type": "token", "content": "Got it! "}
data: {"type": "token", "content": "targeting HR managers "}
data: {"type": "done", "extracted_params": {"geography": ["US"], "target_strategy": "HR managers at Series B SaaS"}}
```

When `type: done` is received, the wizard is complete and `extracted_params` is ready to finalize.

---

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/chat`

Get the full chat history for a campaign session.

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "session_id": "uuid",
  "status": "active",
  "chat_history": [
    {"role": "assistant", "content": "What kind of campaign would you like?"},
    {"role": "user", "content": "Job outreach to startups in India"}
  ],
  "extracted_params": {}
}
```

---

### `PUT /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/sessions`

Finalize the session — save extracted params and trigger the processor background task.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "extracted_params": {
    "geography": ["India", "US"],
    "target_strategy": "CTOs at early-stage startups",
    "purpose": "Looking for backend engineering roles",
    "email_source": "apollo",
    "llm_provider": "openai"
  }
}
```

**Response `202`:**
```json
{
  "message": "Research started. You'll see contacts appear in your dashboard shortly.",
  "campaign_id": "uuid"
}
```

---

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/stream`

SSE stream for real-time processor progress updates.

**Response:** `text/event-stream`

```
data: {"type": "progress", "message": "Found 3 contacts at Stripe"}
data: {"type": "contact_found", "contact": {"company": "Stripe", "email": "hr@stripe.com", "source": "apollo"}}
data: {"type": "draft_ready", "email_job_id": "uuid", "company": "Stripe"}
data: {"type": "complete", "total_contacts": 45, "total_drafts": 45}
```

---

## 4. Outreach Dashboard

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/outreach`

Get all email drafts for a campaign (paginated).

**Query params:** `page=1`, `limit=50`, `status=draft|approved|sent`

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "outreaches": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "contact": {
        "company": "Linear",
        "person_name": "Sarah Chen",
        "email": "sarah@linear.app",
        "title": "Head of Engineering",
        "source": "apollo",
        "bounce_risk": false
      },
      "subject": "Backend Engineer interested in Linear's infra team",
      "body": "Hi Sarah,\n\nI came across Linear's...",
      "draft_status": "pending",
      "status": "draft"
    }
  ],
  "total": 45,
  "page": 1,
  "limit": 50
}
```

---

### `PATCH /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/outreach/{outreach_id}`

Edit subject/body or approve/reject a draft.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "subject": "Updated subject line",
  "body": "Updated body...",
  "draft_status": "approved"
}
```

All fields are optional — send only what's changing.

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "draft_status": "approved",
  "updated_at": "2026-05-06T12:00:00Z"
}
```

---

## 5. Email Jobs (Send)

### `POST /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/email-jobs`

Enqueue emails for sending via background task.

**Request — all approved drafts:**
```json
{
  "outreach_ids": "all"
}
```

**Request — specific IDs:**
```json
{
  "outreach_ids": ["uuid-1", "uuid-2"]
}
```

**Response `202`:**
```json
{
  "queued": 43,
  "skipped_bounced": 2,
  "message": "43 emails queued. Sending at 1 email/sec to stay within Gmail limits."
}
```

---

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/email-jobs`

List all email jobs with their current status.

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "jobs": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "contact_email": "sarah@linear.app",
      "subject": "...",
      "status": "sent",
      "sent_at": "2026-05-06T12:05:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "total": 43
}
```

---

### `PATCH /api/v1/email-jobs/{job_id}`

Manual status override (used for webhook integration in v2 or admin correction).

**Request:**
```json
{
  "status": "bounced"
}
```

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",

# AI Email Outreach Platform — API Contracts

> **Version:** 1.0 (v1 scope)
> All endpoints prefixed `/api/v1`.
> Auth: `Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>` header on all protected routes.
> Content-Type: `application/json` for all request bodies.
> The current user is always derived from the token — never from the request body.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Auth](#1-auth)
2. [Campaigns](#2-campaigns)
3. [Chat (Wizard)](#3-chat-wizard)
4. [Outreach Dashboard](#4-outreach-dashboard)
5. [Email Jobs (Send)](#5-email-jobs-send)

---

## 1. Auth

### `POST /api/v1/auth/google`

Initiate Google OAuth flow.

**Request:** `{}`

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "auth_url": "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth?..."
}
```

---

### `GET /api/v1/auth/google/callback`

Google OAuth redirect handler.

**Query params:** `code`, `state`

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "access_token": "eyJ...",
  "token_type": "bearer",
  "user": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "email": "user@example.com"
  }
}
```

---

## 2. Campaigns

### `POST /api/v1/campaigns`

Create a new campaign and start the chat wizard session.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "type": "job_outreach"
}
```

`type` enum: `job_outreach | b2b | hiring | pr | fundraising | partnership`

**Response `201`:**
```json
{
  "campaign_id": "uuid",
  "session_id": "uuid",
  "status": "draft",
  "first_message": "I'd love to help you with your job outreach campaign! Let's start — what role are you targeting and which companies interest you?"
}
```

---

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns`

List all campaigns for the authenticated user.

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "campaigns": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "type": "job_outreach",
      "status": "ready",
      "created_at": "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "total": 5
}
```

---

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}`

Get campaign detail.

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "type": "b2b",
  "status": "processing",
  "config_json": {},
  "created_at": "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z"
}
```

---

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/stats`

Aggregate stats for a campaign.

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "total_contacts": 45,
  "total_drafts": 45,
  "approved": 43,
  "queued": 2,
  "sent": 38,
  "opened": 12,
  "replied": 3,
  "bounced": 2,
  "failed": 1
}
```

---

## 3. Chat (Wizard)

### `POST /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/chat`

Send a chat message to the campaign wizard. Returns a streaming SSE response.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "message": "I want to reach out to HR managers at Series B SaaS companies in the US"
}
```

**Response:** `text/event-stream`

```
data: {"type": "token", "content": "Got it! "}
data: {"type": "token", "content": "targeting HR managers "}
data: {"type": "done", "extracted_params": {"geography": ["US"], "target_strategy": "HR managers at Series B SaaS"}}
```

When `type: done` is received, the wizard is complete and `extracted_params` is ready to finalize.

---

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/chat`

Get the full chat history for a campaign session.

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "session_id": "uuid",
  "status": "active",
  "chat_history": [
    {"role": "assistant", "content": "What kind of campaign would you like?"},
    {"role": "user", "content": "Job outreach to startups in India"}
  ],
  "extracted_params": {}
}
```

---

### `PUT /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/sessions`

Finalize the session — save extracted params and trigger the processor background task.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "extracted_params": {
    "geography": ["India", "US"],
    "target_strategy": "CTOs at early-stage startups",
    "purpose": "Looking for backend engineering roles",
    "email_source": "apollo",
    "llm_provider": "openai"
  }
}
```

**Response `202`:**
```json
{
  "message": "Research started. You'll see contacts appear in your dashboard shortly.",
  "campaign_id": "uuid"
}
```

---

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/stream`

SSE stream for real-time processor progress updates.

**Response:** `text/event-stream`

```
data: {"type": "progress", "message": "Found 3 contacts at Stripe"}
data: {"type": "contact_found", "contact": {"company": "Stripe", "email": "hr@stripe.com", "source": "apollo"}}
data: {"type": "draft_ready", "email_job_id": "uuid", "company": "Stripe"}
data: {"type": "complete", "total_contacts": 45, "total_drafts": 45}
```

---

## 4. Outreach Dashboard

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/outreach`

Get all email drafts for a campaign (paginated).

**Query params:** `page=1`, `limit=50`, `status=draft|approved|sent`

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "outreaches": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "contact": {
        "company": "Linear",
        "person_name": "Sarah Chen",
        "email": "sarah@linear.app",
        "title": "Head of Engineering",
        "source": "apollo",
        "bounce_risk": false
      },
      "subject": "Backend Engineer interested in Linear's infra team",
      "body": "Hi Sarah,\n\nI came across Linear's...",
      "draft_status": "pending",
      "status": "draft"
    }
  ],
  "total": 45,
  "page": 1,
  "limit": 50
}
```

---

### `PATCH /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/outreach/{outreach_id}`

Edit subject/body or approve/reject a draft.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "subject": "Updated subject line",
  "body": "Updated body...",
  "draft_status": "approved"
}
```

All fields are optional — send only what's changing.

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "draft_status": "approved",
  "updated_at": "2026-05-06T12:00:00Z"
}
```

---

## 5. Email Jobs (Send)

### `POST /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/email-jobs`

Enqueue emails for sending via background task.

**Request — all approved drafts:**
```json
{
  "outreach_ids": "all"
}
```

**Request — specific IDs:**
```json
{
  "outreach_ids": ["uuid-1", "uuid-2"]

```

**Response `202`:**
```json
{
  "queued": 43,
  "skipped_bounced": 2,
  "message": "43 emails queued. Sending at 1 email/sec to stay within Gmail limits."

```

---

### `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/email-jobs`

List all email jobs with their current status.

**Response `200`:**
```json
{
  "jobs": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "contact_email": "sarah@linear.app",
      "subject": "...",

### `PATCH /api/v1/email-jobs/{job_id}`

```json

  "status": "bounced"

```

**Response `200`:**

  "id": "uuid",
  "status": "bounced",
  "updated_at": "2026-05-06T12:10:00Z"
}
```

---

### `POST /api/v1/email-jobs/{job_id}/send`

Manually trigger send for a single email job (adds to background task queue).

Done. Here's what changed:

**`doc.md` — structural changes:**
- **Redis removed** from tech stack, env vars, Docker Compose, and all references
- **ARQ removed** — replaced with FastAPI `BackgroundTasks` throughout
- **New section 5: Design Principles** — explicit SOLID breakdown with code examples for each principle (SRP, OCP, LSP, ISP, DIP), plus the Repository pattern and the "async all the way down" rule
- **New `interfaces/` and `providers/` directories** in project structure — enforces OCP/DIP: new LLM/email/contact providers are additions, not modifications
- **New `repositories/` directory** — `AsyncBaseRepository[T]` base class, services never touch SQLAlchemy directly
- **New `tasks/` directory** — replaces `workers/`, uses `asyncio.Semaphore` for bounded concurrency
- **In-process SSE** — `asyncio.Queue` per campaign instead of Redis pub-sub; designed so it swaps to Redis in v2 behind the same interface
- **P3 build order items** added for the v2 migration path (Redis pub-sub, ARQ) — so the upgrade path is explicit

**`contracts.md` — new file:**
- All API contracts extracted here, doc.md just references it

content.text:
The review was interrupted before I got through any of the four sections (Architecture → Code Quality → Tests → Performance) — we only ran the preamble, then pivoted to rewriting the doc.

Now that the spec is updated, I can run the full review on the current version. But since I rewrote it myself, I can give you a quick honest assessment of what I'd flag:

**Solid:**
- SOLID + Repository pattern is well-structured — the `interfaces/` + `providers/` split is clean
- Async-all-the-way is correct and explicit
- BackgroundTasks swap-out path to ARQ in v2 is low-friction (same interface)
- Build order is sensible

**Things a proper review would challenge:**

1. **In-process SSE is fragile** — `asyncio.Queue` registry lives in memory. If uvicorn restarts mid-campaign (crash, deploy), the client's SSE stream dies and there's no reconnect story. No retry/resume mechanism is documented.

2. **Daily send limit has no persistence** — `check_daily_limit` is described as a DB check but there's no `daily_send_counts` table or column in the schema. Currently undefined.

3. **BackgroundTask DB session** — the `db_factory` pattern for tasks needs to be spelled out in `deps.py` or it'll be implemented inconsistently across tasks.

4. **No test plan** — zero test coverage spec for the processor task, SSE events, or LLM fallback chain. The test files listed (`test_campaigns.py`, `test_chat.py`, `test_llm_service.py`) have no detail on what they cover.

5. **`gmail_oauth_token` in `users` table is unencrypted** — spec says `sendgrid_api_key` is "encrypted at rest" but the Gmail token (which has full send access) has no such note.

Want me to run the full formal review now on the updated doc, or address any of these specific gaps first?

content.text:
# Schedule Remote Agents

You are helping the user schedule, update, list, or run **remote** Claude Code agents. These are NOT local cron jobs — each routine spawns a fully isolated remote session (CCR) in Anthropic's cloud infrastructure, either on a recurring cron schedule or once at a specific time. The agent runs in a sandboxed environment with its own git checkout, tools, and optional MCP connections.

## First Step

Your FIRST action must be a single AskUserQuestion tool call (no preamble). Use this EXACT string for the `question` field — do not paraphrase or shorten it:

"⚠ Heads-up:\n- Not in a git repo — you'll need to specify a repo URL manually (or skip repos entirely).\n\nWhat would you like to do with scheduled remote agents?"

Set `header: "Action"` and offer the four actions (create/list/update/run) as options. After the user picks, follow the matching workflow below.


## What You Can Do

Use the `RemoteTrigger` tool (load it first with `ToolSearch select:RemoteTrigger`; auth is handled in-process — do not use curl):

- `{action: "list"}` — list all routines
- `{action: "get", trigger_id: "..."}` — fetch one routine
- `{action: "create", body: {...}}` — create a routine
- `{action: "update", trigger_id: "...", body: {...}}` — partial update
- `{action: "run", trigger_id: "..."}` — run a routine now

(Note: the API uses `trigger_id` as the parameter name, but the user-facing term is "routine".)

You CANNOT delete routines. If the user asks to delete, direct them to: https://claude.ai/code/routines

## Create body shape

For a recurring schedule:

```json
{
  "name": "AGENT_NAME",
  "cron_expression": "CRON_EXPR",
  "enabled": true,
  "job_config": {
    "ccr": {
      "environment_id": "ENVIRONMENT_ID",
      "session_context": {
        "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
        "sources": [
          {"git_repository": {"url": "https://github.com/ORG/REPO"}}
        ],
        "allowed_tools": ["Bash", "Read", "Write", "Edit", "Glob", "Grep"]
      },
      "events": [
        {"data": {
          "uuid": "<lowercase v4 uuid>",
          "session_id": "",
          "type": "user",
          "parent_tool_use_id": null,
          "message": {"content": "PROMPT_HERE", "role": "user"}
        }}
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

For a one-time run, replace `"cron_expression": "CRON_EXPR"` with `"run_once_at": "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ"` (RFC3339 UTC, must be in the future). Everything else is identical.

Generate a fresh lowercase UUID for `events[].data.uuid` yourself.

## Available MCP Connectors

These are the user's currently connected claude.ai MCP connectors:

Connected connectors (available for routines):
- claude.ai Excalidraw (connector_uuid: 38b70c9d-4611-44cd-bd41-4a662bf5a340, name: Excalidraw, url: https://mcp.excalidraw.com/mcp)
- claude.ai Google Drive (connector_uuid: 1279c977-c395-443a-94fb-619c397f416f, name: Google-Drive, url: https://drivemcp.googleapis.com/mcp/v1)

When attaching connectors to a routine, use the `connector_uuid` and `name` shown above (the name is already sanitized to only contain letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores), and the connector's URL. The `name` field in `mcp_connections` must only contain `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]` — dots and spaces are NOT allowed.

**Important:** Infer what services the agent needs from the user's description. For example, if they say "check Datadog and Slack me errors," the agent needs both Datadog and Slack connectors. Cross-reference against the list above and warn if any required service isn't connected. If a needed connector is missing, direct the user to https://claude.ai/customize/connectors to connect it first.

## Environments

Every routine requires an `environment_id` in the job config. This determines where the remote agent runs. Ask the user which environment to use.

Available environments:
- Default (id: env_019vhEpMm7SkyFMK1o1ZvnYn, kind: anthropic_cloud)

Use the `id` value as the `environment_id` in `job_config.ccr.environment_id`.

**Note:** A new environment `Default` (id: `env_019vhEpMm7SkyFMK1o1ZvnYn`) was just created for the user because they had none. Use this id for `job_config.ccr.environment_id` and mention the creation when you confirm the routine config.


## API Field Reference

### Create Routine — Required Fields
- `name` (string) — A descriptive name
- Exactly ONE of:
  - `cron_expression` (string) — 5-field cron in UTC. **Minimum interval is 1 hour.**
  - `run_once_at` (string) — RFC3339 UTC timestamp. Must be in the future. Fires once, then auto-disables.
- `job_config` (object) — Session configuration (see structure above)

### Create Routine — Optional Fields
- `enabled` (boolean, default: true)
- `mcp_connections` (array) — MCP servers to attach:
  ```json
  [{"connector_uuid": "uuid", "name": "server-name", "url": "https://..."}]
  ```

### Update Routine — Optional Fields
All fields optional (partial update):
- `name`, `cron_expression`, `run_once_at`, `enabled`, `job_config`
- `mcp_connections` — Replace MCP connections
- `clear_mcp_connections` (boolean) — Remove all MCP connections

### Cron Expression Examples

The user's local timezone is **Asia/Calcutta**. Cron expressions and `run_once_at` timestamps are always in UTC. When the user says a local time, convert it to UTC but confirm with them: "9am Asia/Calcutta = Xam UTC, so the cron would be `0 X * * 1-5`." For one-time runs, the same conversion applies — "run this at 3pm" → `"run_once_at": "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:00:00Z"` with their 3pm converted to UTC.

- `0 9 * * 1-5` — Every weekday at 9am **UTC**
- `0 */2 * * *` — Every 2 hours
- `0 0 * * *` — Daily at midnight **UTC**
- `30 14 * * 1` — Every Monday at 2:30pm **UTC**
- `0 8 1 * *` — First of every month at 8am **UTC**

Minimum interval is 1 hour. `*/30 * * * *` will be rejected.

### Current Time (for one-off runs)

When /schedule was invoked it was **Wed, May 6, 2026 at 11:00 PM** (Asia/Calcutta) / **2026-05-06T17:30:10.686Z** UTC. Treat this as an approximate anchor only — the conversation may have been running for a while since then.

**Before computing any `run_once_at` value, you MUST re-check the current time** by running `date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` via the Bash tool. Do not guess or infer today's date from conversation context. Resolve relative requests ("tomorrow at 9am", "in 3 hours", "next Monday") against the freshly fetched time, then echo the resolved local time AND the UTC timestamp back to the user for confirmation before creating the routine. If the resolved time is already in the past, ask the user to clarify rather than silently rolling forward.

## Workflow

### CREATE a new routine:

1. **Understand the goal** — Ask what they want the remote agent to do. What repo(s)? What task? Remind them that the agent runs remotely — it won't have access to their local machine, local files, or local environment variables.
2. **Craft the prompt** — Help them write an effective agent prompt. Good prompts are:
   - Specific about what to do and what success looks like
   - Clear about which files/areas to focus on
   - Explicit about what actions to take (open PRs, commit, just analyze, etc.)
3. **Set the schedule** — Ask when and how often. The user's timezone is Asia/Calcutta. When they say a time (e.g., "every morning at 9am"), assume they mean their local time and convert to UTC for the cron expression. Always confirm the conversion: "9am Asia/Calcutta = Xam UTC." If they want a one-time run (e.g., "once at 3pm", "tomorrow morning", "remind me to check X later"), use `run_once_at` instead of `cron_expression` — same timezone conversion applies. **First re-check the current time with `date -u` via Bash** (the reference time above may be stale in a long conversation), resolve the relative phrase against that fresh value, and confirm the resulting absolute timestamp with the user.
4. **Choose the model** — Default to `claude-sonnet-4-6`. Tell the user which model you're defaulting to and ask if they want a different one.
5. **Validate connections** — Infer what services the agent will need from the user's description. For example, if they say "check Datadog and Slack me errors," the agent needs both Datadog and Slack MCP connectors. Cross-reference with the connectors list above. If any are missing, warn the user and link them to https://claude.ai/customize/connectors to connect first. Ask which git repos the remote agent needs cloned into its environment.
6. **Review and confirm** — Show the full configuration before creating. Let them adjust.
7. **Create it** — Call `RemoteTrigger` with `action: "create"` and show the result. The response includes the routine ID. Always output a link at the end: `https://claude.ai/code/routines/{ROUTINE_ID}`

### UPDATE a routine:

1. List routines first so they can pick one
2. Ask what they want to change
3. Show current vs proposed value
4. Confirm and update

### LIST routines:

1. Fetch and display in a readable format
2. Show: name, schedule (human-readable), enabled/disabled, next run, repo(s)

### RUN NOW:

1. List routines if they haven't specified which one
2. Confirm which routine
3. Execute and confirm

## Important Notes

- These are REMOTE agents — they run in Anthropic's cloud, not on the user's machine. They cannot access local files, local services, or local environment variables.
- Always convert cron to human-readable when displaying
- When listing routines, `ended_reason: "run_once_fired"` means a one-shot already ran (shows as "Ran" in the web UI). The user can re-arm it by updating with a new `run_once_at`.
- Default to `enabled: true` unless user says otherwise
- Accept GitHub URLs in any format (https://github.com/org/repo, org/repo, etc.) and normalize to the full HTTPS URL (without .git suffix)
- The prompt is the most important part — spend time getting it right. The remote agent starts with zero context, so the prompt must be self-contained.
- To delete a routine, direct users to https://claude.ai/code/routines
